LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JULIE LAKE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT RAISED: 1) WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION AND IS A PROPER PARTY PLAINTIFF. (KRAYZMAN DECLARATION, EXHIBITS 1 & 2.) 2) WHETHER AN AGREEMENT WAS FORMED BASED ON PRIOR TRANSACTIONS BETWEEN THE PARTIES THAT THE ACCOUNT TERMS ARE TRUE AND THE BALANCE IS DUE AND OWING. (KRAYZMAN DECLARATION, EXHIBIT 4, 5, AND 6.) 3) WHETHER THE ASSIGNEE OF THE ACCOUNT IS FEDERAL CREDIT CORP. OR CRAYZMAN. (COUNSEL TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-07-2010 Dismissal Or Judgment On File As To All Defendants

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-11-2010 Set For Case Management Conference On Aug-06-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 212

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JULIE LAKE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Chirnese Liverpool, Esq. Attorney at Law, (818) 207-1074 appearing through Courtcall for Plaintiff, and opposing party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, triable issues of material fact raised: 1) Whether plaintiff has standing to maintain this action and is a proper party plaintiff. (Krayzman declaration, Exhibits 1 & 2.) 2) Whether an agreement was formed based on prior transactions between the parties that the account terms are true and the balance is due and owing. (Krayzman declaration, Exhibit 4, 5, and 6.) 3) Whether the assignee of the account is Federal credit Corp. or Crayzman. (Counsel to prepare proposed Order for the Court's signature.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Continued To Mar-04-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JULIE LAKE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: continued to March 4, 2010 due to late opposition. Reply due February 24, 2010. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797
